phase_name: Spec alignment, bug fixes, and code quality hardening
goal: Address all gaps found during a cross-cutting review of specs/, claude/rules/, and src/. Tasks fall into four buckets: (1) bugs where the implementation diverges from the spec's stated behaviour, (2) code-quality issues that violate the Rust/Python rules, (3) spec-alignment fixes where the code is correct but the spec is silent or wrong, and (4) test coverage gaps.

tasks.title: SIGINT in CLI mode does not kill `llm.py` subprocess
tasks.description:
specs/cli.md states: 'User sends SIGINT (Ctrl-C) during LLM streaming → Kill llm.py subprocess immediately, delete any temp WAV file, exit 130.'

The SIGINT handler in `src/main.rs` only stores the WAV path (via `wav_for_sigint: Arc<Mutex<Option<PathBuf>>>`). The `llm_child` process handle is a local variable and is never shared with the signal handler, so pressing Ctrl-C during LLM streaming exits the Rust process but leaves `llm.py` running as an orphan.

Fix: introduce a second shared slot `Arc<Mutex<Option<process::Child>>>` (analogous to `cli_active_child` used for transcription) and store `llm_child` in it before streaming. The SIGINT handler should call `.kill()` on any process stored in this slot before exiting 130.

Pattern is already established by `cli_active_child` in `transcription.rs` — apply the same pattern for the LLM child in `main.rs`.
checks: While streaming LLM output in CLI mode, press Ctrl-C — `llm.py` process is no longer visible in `ps aux` after exit.

tasks.title: `transcribe.py` always prints 'first use only' model download warning
tasks.description:
Every call to `transcribe()` in `src/core/transcribe.py` unconditionally prints:

```
Downloading Whisper {model_name} model — first use only (may take 1–3 minutes)…
```

The message says 'first use only', but it appears on every transcription — even after the Whisper model is cached in `~/.cache/whisper/`. This is misleading.

Fix options (pick the simplest):
1. Remove the print statement entirely — Whisper prints its own progress output when downloading; the Rust binary captures/ignores stderr in app mode anyway.
2. Change wording to a neutral 'Loading Whisper {model_name} model…' (removes the misleading 'first use only' claim).

Option 2 is preferred: it gives useful context during CLI use without making a false claim about frequency.
checks: Run transcription twice with the same model — second run message does not say 'first use only'.
python-style.mdc: file still passes Black formatting.

tasks.title: `llm.py` reads stdin before parsing CLI args, blocking `--help`
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, `main()` calls `sys.stdin.read()` as its first statement, before `argparse` parses `--prompt-file` or `--help`:

```python
def main() -> None:
    transcript = sys.stdin.read()   # ← blocks here, waiting for EOF
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(...)
    args = parser.parse_args()
```

Running `python3 src/core/llm.py --help` hangs until the user presses Ctrl-D, because stdin.read() blocks waiting for EOF before argparse ever runs.

specs/llm.md shows the canonical order: parse args first, then read stdin.

Fix: move `transcript = sys.stdin.read()` to after `args = parser.parse_args()`, matching the spec and the pattern used in development/debugging.

Note: this does not affect production usage (Rust writes the transcript then closes stdin before Python starts reading), but it matters for manual testing and `--help`.
checks: `python3 src/core/llm.py --help` prints help text immediately without waiting for Ctrl-D.
python-style.mdc: file still passes Black formatting.

tasks.title: Step comment numbering in `main.rs` jumps from 7 to 14
Remove unused `tempfile` crate from `Cargo.toml`
tasks.description:
`Cargo.toml` lists `tempfile = "3"` as a dependency. No Rust source file imports or uses it — confirmed by `grep -r 'use tempfile\|tempfile::' src/*.rs` returning no matches.

`audio.rs` constructs the temp WAV path via `std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("yaptap_{timestamp}.wav"))` (stdlib only). The `tempfile` crate was originally required by the spec (`specs/audio-recording.md` says 'Created with `tempfile::Builder`') but the implementation diverged and uses stdlib instead.

Fix: remove `tempfile = "3"` from `[dependencies]` in `Cargo.toml`.

Note: this also requires updating `specs/audio-recording.md` to remove the `tempfile::Builder` reference (covered by P12-T014).

tasks.title: Deduplicate `save_hotkey` and `save_prompt` in `config.rs`
checks: Atomic write path covered: prompt and hotkey saves still persist correctly on disk.
The EXDEV fallback logic appears only once in `config.rs`.

tasks.title: Config file initial write missing user-helpful comment header
tasks.description:
specs/config.md shows the config file with comments that explain each field:

```toml
# YapTap configuration
# hotkey: change via in-app dialog (takes effect immediately), or edit here and restart
# whisper_model, llm_model: edit here and restart to apply

hotkey = "option+space"    # global hotkey to start/stop recording
selected_prompt = ""       # stem of selected prompt file; empty = No Prompt
whisper_model = "base"     # Whisper model for transcription
llm_model = "llama3"       # Ollama model for LLM inference
```

`config.rs` uses `toml::to_string_pretty(&default_cfg)` which produces bare key-value lines with no comments. New users who open the config file see no guidance.

Fix: replace the `toml::to_string_pretty` call in `AppConfig::load()` (the 'write defaults' branch) with a hardcoded string literal that matches the spec exactly. The literal should be maintained alongside the `AppConfig` struct definition.
checks: Delete `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml`, launch app, open `~/.config/yaptap/config.toml` — file contains the comment header.

tasks.title: Add `#!/usr/bin/env python3` shebang to `transcribe.py`
tasks.description:
specs/transcription.md shows `transcribe.py` with a shebang on line 1:
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
```

The actual `src/core/transcribe.py` starts with a docstring, no shebang. This means the file cannot be executed directly (`./transcribe.py <wav>`) without explicitly invoking `python3`. Add the shebang as the first line.

tasks.title: Add `logging.basicConfig` to `transcribe.py` `main()`
tasks.description:
specs/transcription.md shows `main()` calling `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, stream=sys.stderr)` before any other logic. The actual `main()` in `src/core/transcribe.py` has no `logging.basicConfig` call.

Without this, the `log = logging.getLogger(__name__)` logger at module level produces no output even when warnings or errors are logged via `log.warning()` / `log.error()` — they are silently discarded by the root logger's default NullHandler.

Fix: add `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, stream=sys.stderr)` as the first statement of `main()` in `transcribe.py`.
checks: Running `transcribe.py` with `PYTHONPATH` and an invalid path produces log output on stderr.

tasks.title: Add `logging.basicConfig` to `llm.py` `main()`
tasks.description:
specs/llm.md shows `main()` calling `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, stream=sys.stderr)`. The actual `main()` in `src/core/llm.py` has no such call, meaning `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at module level produces no output.

Fix: add `logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING, stream=sys.stderr)` as the first statement of `main()` in `llm.py`, immediately before the `argparse` setup (and after moving stdin read per P12-T003).

tasks.title: Hotkey parser rejects punctuation/symbol keys — spec promises 'any printable character'
tasks.description:
specs/config.md states under 'Valid keys':
> Any printable character — e.g. `a`, `1`, `,`, `/`

`parse_key()` in `src/hotkey.rs` only handles:
- Letters a–z (mapped to `rdev::Key::KeyA` … `rdev::Key::KeyZ`)
- Digits 0–9 (mapped to `rdev::Key::Num0` … `rdev::Key::Num9`)
- Named keys (space, tab, return, escape, delete, arrows, f1–f20)

Punctuation keys such as `,`, `.`, `/`, `;`, `'`, `[`, `]`, `\`, `-`, `=` are silently rejected and produce an 'unrecognised key' error. This breaks the spec promise.

Fix: add rdev key mappings for the full set of printable punctuation keys available on a standard US keyboard. Add them to `parse_key()` as additional single-character match arms, and add corresponding unit tests in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.

Priority keys to add: `,` → `rdev::Key::Comma`, `.` → `rdev::Key::Dot`, `/` → `rdev::Key::Slash`, `;` → `rdev::Key::SemiColon`, `'` → `rdev::Key::Quote`, `[` → `rdev::Key::LeftBracket`, `]` → `rdev::Key::RightBracket`, `\` → `rdev::Key::BackSlash`, `-` → `rdev::Key::Minus`, `=` → `rdev::Key::Equal`, `` ` `` → `rdev::Key::BackQuote`.
checks: `cargo test` passes (new tests for each new key).

tasks.title: Update `specs/menubar.md` to document 'Start/Stop Recording' menu item
tasks.description:
The implementation (`src/app.rs`, `build_menu()`) includes a 'Start Recording' / 'Stop Recording' / 'Processing…' toggle as the first item in the dropdown menu — above the prompt list. This item was added in PRD_3 (P3-T076) but was never added to `specs/menubar.md`.

The current spec shows the menu as:
```
   Action Items
✓  Email Reply
   ...
   ─────────────────────────
   No Prompt
   ...
```

The actual menu is:
```
   Start Recording           ← not in spec
   ─────────────────────────
   Action Items
✓  Email Reply
   ...
   ─────────────────────────
   No Prompt
   ...
```

Update the menu structure diagram in `specs/menubar.md` to include the toggle item and describe its behaviour: enabled in Idle (shows 'Start Recording') and Recording states (shows 'Stop Recording'), disabled and shows 'Processing…' during Processing state.
checks: `specs/menubar.md` menu structure diagram includes the toggle row.
Description of toggle item behaviour (enabled/disabled by state) is present.

tasks.title: Update `specs/packaging.md` pip install and venv repair to reflect PRD_11 changes
tasks.description:
`specs/packaging.md` documents the first-launch setup sequence as:
```sh
python3 -m venv ~/.config/yaptap/.venv
~/.config/yaptap/.venv/bin/pip install --quiet openai-whisper ollama
```

After PRD_11, the actual install command is:
```sh
pip install --quiet numpy<2 openai-whisper ollama tomli
```

Additionally, PRD_11 introduced `venv_healthy()` (two-stage repair: pip-in-place first, full teardown only on failure) which is not described in the spec.

Update `specs/packaging.md`:
1. Add `numpy<2` and `tomli` to the pip install line in the 'Setup Sequence' section.
2. Add a 'Venv Health Check and Repair' sub-section describing the two-stage repair: on launch, if the venv exists but `venv_healthy()` fails, attempt `pip install --quiet numpy<2 tomli` in-place; only tear down and re-run full setup if pip repair fails.

tasks.title: Update `specs/packaging.md` Makefile spec to include `install` target (added in PRD_11)
tasks.description:
The Makefile spec in `specs/packaging.md` shows the `.PHONY` line as:
```makefile
.PHONY: build icns app dmg clean
```

After PRD_11-T005, the actual Makefile has `install` added:
```makefile
.PHONY: build icns app install dmg clean
```

The spec is missing the `install` target definition and its entry in the 'Target summary' table.

Update `specs/packaging.md`:
1. Add `install` to the `.PHONY` line.
2. Add the `install` target definition (copies `dist/YapTap.app` to `/Applications/`).
3. Add a row to the 'Target summary' table: `make install | app | /Applications/YapTap.app`.
checks: Target summary table has an `install` row.

tasks.title: Update `specs/architecture.md` and `specs/audio-recording.md` — `tempfile` crate is unused
tasks.description:
`specs/architecture.md` lists `tempfile` under 'Dependencies (phases 1 & 2) / Rust'. `specs/audio-recording.md` says 'Created with `tempfile::Builder` in the system temp directory.'

As established in P12-T005, the `tempfile` Rust crate is not imported or used in any `.rs` file. `audio.rs` uses `std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("yaptap_{timestamp}.wav"))` instead.

Update both specs:
1. `specs/architecture.md`: remove `tempfile` from the Rust dependencies list.
2. `specs/audio-recording.md`: replace 'Created with `tempfile::Builder` in the system temp directory' with 'Created via `std::env::temp_dir()` in the system temp directory, named `yaptap_<unix_timestamp>.wav`'.

tasks.title: Menu bar icon invisible on dark menu bar — template image API not available in tray-icon 0.14
tasks.description:
specs/menubar.md requires the menu bar icon to be a 'macOS template image (monochrome; the system adapts it for light and dark menu bar themes automatically).'

tray-icon 0.14 exposes no public API to mark `NSStatusItem`'s `NSImage` as a template image. The current icons are black-on-transparent PNGs and are invisible when the macOS menu bar is set to dark mode.

This is tracked in-code as `// FIXME(P5-I002)` in `src/app.rs`.

Options:
1. **Upgrade tray-icon**: check if a newer version (0.15+) exposes `NSImage.setTemplate(true)`. If so, upgrade and call the API.
2. **Direct NSStatusItem access via objc**: use `msg_send!` to reach the `NSStatusItem` and call `[[statusItem button] image] setTemplate:YES`. This is `unsafe` but straightforward.
3. **Use inverted icon on dark mode**: detect `NSAppearance` and load a white-on-transparent icon variant.

Option 2 is recommended as the lowest-effort correct solution. Option 1 should be checked first.

This task remains 'to do' until the template image is applied; the FIXME comment must be removed when resolved.
checks: Set macOS menu bar appearance to Dark — tray icon is visible.
Set macOS menu bar appearance to Light — tray icon is still visible.

tasks.title: Add 'What/Why' docstrings to `transcribe_test.py` test methods
tasks.description:
claude/rules/python-unittest.mdc states: 'every test docstring must explain **what** and **why**'.

`src/core/transcribe_test.py` has 5 test methods; only one has a docstring (single-line, no 'Why'). The other four have no docstring at all:

| Method | Current docstring |
|--------|------------------|
| `test_valid_wav_returns_transcript` | One-liner (no Why) |
| `test_empty_wav_path_raises_value_error` | None |
| `test_missing_wav_file_raises_value_error` | None |
| `test_model_load_failure_raises_runtime_error` | None |
| `test_transcription_failure_raises_runtime_error` | None |

Add two-paragraph docstrings to all five tests matching the pattern used in `llm_test.py` (explicit 'What is being tested:' and 'Why this test matters:' lines).
checks: Each of the five test methods has a docstring with both 'What' and 'Why' explanations.
